summary: Comprehensive frontend testing completed for Django car damage assessment app. All critical issues from iteration 1 have been resolved. CSRF authentication flow now works perfectly, and all core functionality is operational with excellent mobile responsiveness.

resolved_from_previous.issue: CSRF token validation failing with 403 Forbidden
resolved_from_previous.details: Form submissions now work correctly - CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS configuration fixed
login_required_custom decorator now properly redirects unauthenticated users

resolved_from_previous.flow: Phone number submission and SMS verification
resolved_from_previous.issue: Form submission blocked by CSRF 403 error
resolved_from_previous.details: Complete authentication flow now working seamlessly

passed_tests: Complete authentication flow: phone input → SMS code 1234 → damage form access
Phone number validation and formatting (+7 format accepted)
SMS verification with mocked code 1234 works perfectly
VIN input validation (correctly requires exactly 17 characters)
Description text area accepts damage details in Russian
Photo upload requirement validation (prevents submission without photos)
Bottom navigation between all three tabs (Новая, Расчёты, Контакты)
Calculations page displays correct empty state ('Пока нет расчётов')
Contacts page shows complete information (phone: +7 495 123-45-67, address: Москва)
Logout functionality redirects to phone input correctly
Red-white minimalist design theme confirmed (button color: rgb(220, 38, 38))
Mobile responsive design - navigation remains accessible on mobile (390x844)
Session management and user state persistence across page navigation
All form validations display appropriate Russian error messages

success_percentage.frontend: 95% (All UI components functional, complete user flow operational)
